log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a UPX-compressed PE32 executable tagged as a 'spreader'. It's 517KB. I've already seen significant IOC data from the initial overview (DNS to i2.tietuku.com, XOR encoding, keylogging, sc
IOC summary confirms: 1 unique IP, 1 domain (i2.tietuku.com), 2 dropped files, 1 process created, no persistence keys found yet. The sample shows strong indicators of a keylogger/spyware (keylogging,

Network activity shows only DNS lookup to i2.tietuku.com and no HTTP conversations recorded, despite CAPA detecting HTTP request capabilities. This might be due to timing or sandbox limitations. The s
Two dropped files with similar names (Syslambxdjz.exe and Syslembxdjz.exe) and different hashes. This suggests the malware might be unpacking or creating variants. I need to check registry changes to
No registry persistence keys were found, which is interesting for a keylogger/spyware. It might use other persistence mechanisms or rely on user interaction. I need to examine process activity to see
